reason.Diploma: A nursing diploma is registrable in most destinations, though a degree eases UK and Australian pathways.
reason.Degree: A nursing degree is recognised internationally and meets most overseas registration bars.
reason.Honours: An Honours-level nursing qualification is well regarded internationally.
reason.Masters: A Masters-level qualification supports senior and specialist posts abroad.
reason.Other: A non-standard qualification may need credential evaluation before overseas registration.

reason.Active: Active SANC registration is in good standing, which overseas councils require for verification.
reason.Pending renewal: Your SANC renewal is in progress, clear it before applying as councils verify current standing.
reason.Lapsed: Lapsed SANC registration must be renewed before overseas councils will verify your status.

reason.Completed: Completed overseas registration with NMC, AHPRA, or NCNZ puts you ready to be hired.
reason.In progress: Overseas registration is underway, keep the process moving as it is the main timeline driver.
reason.None started: No overseas registration started yet, NMC, AHPRA, and NCNZ all require it before you can practise.

reason.ICU: ICU nurses are in acute global shortage, expect hiring priority and strong salary leverage.
reason.Theatre: Theatre nurses are highly sought after across the UK and Gulf, a significant advantage.
reason.Emergency: Emergency nursing is a critical shortage area abroad, very strong demand.
reason.Maternity: Maternity and midwifery nurses are scarce worldwide, strong leverage in most markets.
reason.General: General nursing is widely needed and gives you solid, broad demand abroad.
reason.Paediatrics: Paediatric nursing has steady demand, particularly in the UK and Gulf.
reason.Psychiatry: Mental health nursing is needed in the UK and Australia, solid demand.
reason.Other: Confirm how your speciality maps to overseas roles, demand varies by destination.

rules.empty_reason: No speciality recorded. Shortage specialities such as ICU, Theatre, Emergency, and Maternity get hiring priority and often a salary premium, so add yours.

ranges.reason: Under 2 years post-qualifying, most overseas employers require at least 2 years of post-registration experience.
2 to 5 years experience meets the bar for most overseas nursing roles.
5 to 10 years experience makes you a strong applicant for competitive posts.
10+ years experience marks you as a senior-level applicant.

reason.true: Prior overseas nursing experience significantly strengthens your applications.
reason.false: No overseas experience yet, workable but recruiters favour nurses with international exposure.

reason.Valid — 2+ years remaining: Your passport is valid and meets typical work visa requirements.
reason.Valid — under 2 years remaining: Your passport is valid but renew it soon, most work visas need 2+ years remaining.
reason.Expired: Your passport is expired, renew it before applying.
reason.No passport: You have no passport, apply immediately as this is the first blocker.

reason.Current (within 6 months): Your police clearance is current and meets the visa-ready bar.
reason.Older than 6 months: Your police clearance is older than 6 months, most visa applications require a fresh one.
reason.None: You have no police clearance, apply now as SAPS turnaround is typically 4 to 8 weeks.

reason.Under R30k: Below the typical pre-departure budget, registration exams and visa fees add up quickly.
reason.R30k – R70k: Covers basic pre-departure costs but tight once registration and relocation fees stack up.
reason.R70k – R150k: A comfortable pre-departure budget for most nursing destinations.
reason.R150k+: A strong financial position for relocation.
reason.Prefer not to say: Budget not declared, share it privately with us if it shapes your options.

reason.Native / first language: English as a first language meets the language bar for the UK, Australia, and NZ.
reason.Fluent: Fluent English is typically sufficient, but you will still need an IELTS or OET result to register.
reason.Conversational: Conversational English needs strengthening, NMC and AHPRA require evidence via IELTS or OET.
reason.Basic: Basic English is a significant blocker for overseas nursing registration.

reason.IELTS: IELTS is accepted by nursing councils in the UK, Australia, and NZ.
reason.OET: OET is the healthcare-specific test accepted by NMC, AHPRA, and NCNZ.
reason.Not yet: No English test taken yet, NMC and AHPRA both require IELTS or OET before registration.

reason.As soon as possible: An ASAP timeline is realistic once registration and English tests are in hand, start now.
reason.Within 6 months: A 6-month horizon is workable provided your registration and documents are progressing.
reason.Within 1 year: A 1-year horizon is ideal for completing overseas registration and visa processing.
reason.Just exploring: Exploring only, no urgency means you can plan the registration steps strategically.
